mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in more than 65% of periods when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 4.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, which triggers risk-off positioning and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in over 70% of instances when UNRATE crosses above 4.5%, or the signal shows zero predictive power in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which drives corporate revenue growth expectations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or the interaction term loses statistical significance with a p-value greater than 0.10 in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which supports near-term corporate earnings revisions and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to precede positive 5-day S&P 500 returns during recovery phases, or the signal reverses direction when inflation expectations exceed 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 205.0 indicate entrenched inflationary pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of periods when CPIAUCSL remains above 205.0, or the signal loses predictive accuracy when real yields turn negative.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects a resilient labor market that sustains household income and consumption spending, which supports corporate earnings trajectories and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS increases consistently precede negative 60-day S&P 500 returns, or the correlation between payroll growth and equity returns turns negative over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above trend confirms macroeconomic expansion, which reduces recession probability and encourages equity risk-taking, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days following data release.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during early-cycle phases, or the signal magnitude drops below statistical significance when market volatility exceeds the 80th percentile.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in industrial production and employment demonstrates synchronized supply-side and labor market strength, which validates economic resilience forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative returns when either series decouples, or the interaction effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing with a p-value greater than 0.15.
